module-9-greenhouse-sensor-project.label: Module 9 Greenhouse Sensor Project
module-9-greenhouse-sensor-project.summary: Build a greenhouse monitor with an abstract Sensor contract, child constructors that call `super()`, and one polymorphic report.

greenhouse_project_step_1_base_and_tempe.title: Create an abstract Sensor and one concrete child
greenhouse_project_step_1_base_and_tempe.prompt: Create the shared `Sensor(ABC)` base class with a constructor that stores `zone` and an abstract `reading()` method. Then complete `TemperatureSensor`: call `super().__init__(zone)`, store `celsius`, and return `Temperature: <zone> = <value> C` from `reading()`. Run `main.py` to print the North Bed reading.
greenhouse_project_step_1_base_and_tempe.hint: Define the abstract contract first, then make TemperatureSensor concrete by implementing reading().

help.concept: The ABC makes `reading()` a required method. `super().__init__(zone)` reuses the parent setup instead of copying it.
help.hint_2: Implement reading() in TemperatureSensor so Python can instantiate it.

greenhouse_project_step_1_base_and_tempe.starterCode: # TODO: import TemperatureSensor, create the North Bed sensor, and print reading().

main_py.content:
# TODO: import TemperatureSensor from models.temperature_sensor.
# Create TemperatureSensor("North Bed", 21.5) and print sensor.reading().

models___init___py.content: # Package marker for greenhouse model imports.

greenhouse_project_step_1_base_and_tempe.solutionCode:
from models.temperature_sensor import TemperatureSensor

sensor = TemperatureSensor("North Bed", 21.5)
print(sensor.reading())

main_py.content:
from models.temperature_sensor import TemperatureSensor

sensor = TemperatureSensor("North Bed", 21.5)
print(sensor.reading())

models___init___py.content: # Package marker for greenhouse model imports.

0.message: Define the Sensor abstract base class.

1.message: Keep Sensor abstract instead of creating Sensor objects directly.

2.message: Store the shared zone in the parent constructor.

3.message: Define TemperatureSensor as a concrete Sensor child.

4.message: TemperatureSensor should accept zone and celsius.

5.message: TemperatureSensor should store its state and return the expected reading.

greenhouse_project_step_2_add_humidity_o.title: Add HumiditySensor and override reading()
greenhouse_project_step_2_add_humidity_o.prompt: Continue from the working base and temperature sensor. Override `TemperatureSensor.reading()` so it returns `Temperature: <zone> = <celsius> C`. Add `HumiditySensor` with a `percent` value and its own `reading()` result: `Humidity: <zone> = <percent>%`. Print both readings from `main.py`.
greenhouse_project_step_2_add_humidity_o.hint: Keep the shared method name `reading()` in both subclasses.

help.concept: Overriding lets each sensor type format its own reading while callers use one method name.
help.hint_1: TemperatureSensor should format its zone and celsius value.
help.hint_2: HumiditySensor should inherit Sensor, store percent, and override reading().

greenhouse_project_step_2_add_humidity_o.starterCode:
# Add HumiditySensor and print both specialized readings.
from models.temperature_sensor import TemperatureSensor

sensor = TemperatureSensor("North Bed", 21.5)
print(sensor.reading())

main_py.content:
# Import HumiditySensor, create both sensors, and print both readings.
from models.temperature_sensor import TemperatureSensor

sensor = TemperatureSensor("North Bed", 21.5)
print(sensor.reading())

models___init___py.content: # Package marker for greenhouse model imports.

models_humidity_sensor_py.content: # Create a HumiditySensor with zone, percent, and reading().

greenhouse_project_step_2_add_humidity_o.solutionCode:
from models.temperature_sensor import TemperatureSensor
from models.humidity_sensor import HumiditySensor

temperature = TemperatureSensor("North Bed", 21.5)
humidity = HumiditySensor("Seedling Bench", 64)
print(temperature.reading())
print(humidity.reading())

main_py.content:
from models.temperature_sensor import TemperatureSensor
from models.humidity_sensor import HumiditySensor

temperature = TemperatureSensor("North Bed", 21.5)
humidity = HumiditySensor("Seedling Bench", 64)
print(temperature.reading())
print(humidity.reading())

models___init___py.content: # Package marker for greenhouse model imports.

0.message: TemperatureSensor.reading() should return the specialized temperature reading.

2.message: HumiditySensor should accept zone and percent.

3.message: HumiditySensor should store zone and percent.

4.message: HumiditySensor.reading() should return the specialized humidity reading.

greenhouse_project_step_3_mixed_sensor_l.title: Build one mixed sensor list
greenhouse_project_step_3_mixed_sensor_l.prompt: Continue from the two working sensor classes. Put a `TemperatureSensor` and a `HumiditySensor` in one `sensors` list. Loop through the list and print `sensor.reading()` for every object. Do not branch on the sensor type.
greenhouse_project_step_3_mixed_sensor_l.hint: Use one list and one loop; the shared reading() method handles both objects.

help.concept: This milestone applies polymorphism to a greenhouse monitoring problem that was not used in the lessons.
help.hint_2: Loop with `for sensor in sensors:` and print `sensor.reading()`.

greenhouse_project_step_3_mixed_sensor_l.starterCode:
from models.temperature_sensor import TemperatureSensor
from models.humidity_sensor import HumiditySensor

sensors = [
    TemperatureSensor("North Bed", 21.5),
    HumiditySensor("Seedling Bench", 64),
]

# TODO: print every reading with one shared loop.

main_py.content:
from models.temperature_sensor import TemperatureSensor
from models.humidity_sensor import HumiditySensor

sensors = [
    TemperatureSensor("North Bed", 21.5),
    HumiditySensor("Seedling Bench", 64),
]

# TODO: loop through sensors and print sensor.reading().

models___init___py.content: # Package marker for greenhouse model imports.

greenhouse_project_step_3_mixed_sensor_l.solutionCode:

from models.humidity_sensor import HumiditySensor

sensors = [
    TemperatureSensor("North Bed", 21.5),
    HumiditySensor("Seedling Bench", 64),
]

for sensor in sensors:

main_py.content:

from models.humidity_sensor import HumiditySensor

sensors = [
    TemperatureSensor("North Bed", 21.5),
    HumiditySensor("Seedling Bench", 64),

for sensor in sensors:
    print(sensor.reading())

0.message: Create at least one TemperatureSensor object.

1.message: Create at least one HumiditySensor object.

2.message: TemperatureSensor.reading() should still return the expected reading.

3.message: HumiditySensor.reading() should still return the expected reading.

4.message: Print both readings from one shared loop.

greenhouse_project_step_4_monitor_class_.title: Add the GreenhouseMonitor report
greenhouse_project_step_4_monitor_class_.prompt: Finish the greenhouse project by adding `GreenhouseMonitor`. It should own a `sensors` list, add sensors with `add_sensor(sensor)`, and print each sensor reading from `print_report()`. Keep `main.py` thin: create the monitor, add both sensors, and print the report.
greenhouse_project_step_4_monitor_class_.hint: The monitor should own the mixed collection instead of main.py managing the loop.

help.concept: The final milestone gives collection ownership to a dedicated class.
help.hint_2: Append in `add_sensor()` and loop over `self.sensors` in `print_report()`.

greenhouse_project_step_4_monitor_class_.starterCode:
# Run this greenhouse demo after completing models/greenhouse_monitor.py.
from models.temperature_sensor import TemperatureSensor
from models.humidity_sensor import HumiditySensor
from models.greenhouse_monitor import GreenhouseMonitor

monitor = GreenhouseMonitor()
monitor.add_sensor(TemperatureSensor("North Bed", 21.5))
monitor.add_sensor(HumiditySensor("Seedling Bench", 64))
monitor.print_report()

main_py.content:
# Run this greenhouse demo after completing models/greenhouse_monitor.py.
from models.temperature_sensor import TemperatureSensor
from models.humidity_sensor import HumiditySensor
from models.greenhouse_monitor import GreenhouseMonitor

monitor = GreenhouseMonitor()
monitor.add_sensor(TemperatureSensor("North Bed", 21.5))
monitor.add_sensor(HumiditySensor("Seedling Bench", 64))
monitor.print_report()

Humidity: Seedling Bench = 64%

module-9-greenhouse-sensor-project-sketc.title: Project brief: greenhouse sensor monitor
module-9-greenhouse-sensor-project-sketc.bodyMarkdown:
A neighborhood greenhouse has temperature and humidity sensors in different growing zones. The staff want one program that can read every sensor through the same method.

This project uses a fresh greenhouse problem while applying the Python tools from the lessons:

1. Define an abstract `Sensor` contract and build `TemperatureSensor` with `super()`.
2. Add `HumiditySensor`, again reusing the parent constructor.
3. Put both concrete sensor types in one list and call `reading()` in one loop.
4. Add `GreenhouseMonitor` to own the collection and print the report.

The result combines an enforceable ABC, shared parent setup, concrete overrides, and polymorphism.
